title: Re-home scout's frontend out of go:embed onto a toolkit-built PWA
userStory: As the user, I want scout's UI rebuilt as a toolkit PWA so that it shares the platform's design system, shell, and PWA plumbing and is installable like the other apps — without changing how the pipeline works.
acceptanceCriteria: A scout web/ PWA is created that depends on the web-toolkit and reproduces today's UI: triage list, company detail panel, jobs/tracker view, run control (with the SSE progress stream), criteria/profile panel, and the playbook/taste editor

The PWA consumes toolkit modules (tokens/base.css, shell, components, pwa, brain client, session) rather than bespoke CSS/JS; the old internal/web/index.html is removed and GET / serves the built PWA dist

The visual result preserves scout's current look — the toolkit's components ARE scout's existing elements harvested (brainbot plan US-002); this is a re-home, not a redesign, and uses NO third-party component library
Scout's Go /api/* behavior is unchanged (no handler signatures/semantics altered); a small /api/brain/* read-only proxy is added for the toolkit brain client if needed

Scout's data layer is untouched: it stays on local SQLite (modernc.org/sqlite), owns its working set, and is NOT moved into the brain or onto the brain's Postgres — per app-platform.md 'App data: two kinds, and where the engines live'
All current interactions still work: start enrich/verdict runs and watch live progress, CSV ingest, capture-by-link, edit company/posting tracking, view trace, refresh profile, edit playbook/taste
scout build still produces a single runnable binary (PWA dist embedded or served), preserving the 'one binary' operations story or documenting the new build step in operations.md

[Verification] Build + scout serve → exercise each view in the browser: triage loads, a verdict run streams progress to completion, jobs view edits persist, editor saves; no console errors; manifest/SW valid (installable)
externalDependencies: brainbot FEAT-20260607_155517-4fd1 US-002 (toolkit extracted) and US-003 (toolkit validated)
